# Upscalify AI

> An AI image and video upscaler from The Atom. It reconstructs real detail in
> low-resolution or low-quality files, producing a sharper, higher-resolution
> result rather than a blurred enlargement.

Upscalify AI is a web application at https://upscalify.theatom.lk. It is part of
The Atom's product family (https://theatom.lk) and was built by Zaki.

## What it does

- Upscales images: an image is analyzed and reconstructed in a single pass to a
  higher resolution with sharper edges and cleaner texture.
- Upscales video: each frame is reconstructed and checked against neighbouring
  frames for temporal consistency, so motion stays steady without flicker. The
  clip is reassembled with its original audio track preserved.
- Before/after comparison: the reconstructed result can be compared directly
  against the original with a draggable divider before downloading.

## Quality options

Two paths are offered, described by outcome rather than by the model that powers
them:

- Fast: roughly doubles resolution and returns in seconds. Suited to sources
  that are already reasonable, or for a quick lift.
- High quality: reconstructs more aggressively at a higher scale, taking longer,
  for small, soft, or noisy sources.

## How it runs

In the current version (v1), all processing happens locally on the user's own
machine against an on-device inference engine. Files are not uploaded to any
third-party service. There is no account, no billing, and no job queue. Results
are written to local storage for download.

A later phase will move the inference layer to a hosted backend for users
without local hardware. The application is built around a single upscale
interface so this change is invisible to the user experience.

## Supported inputs

- Images: JPEG, PNG, WebP, AVIF, TIFF, BMP, GIF (up to 30 MB).
- Video: MP4, MOV, WebM, MKV, AVI (up to 500 MB).

## Pages

- https://upscalify.theatom.lk/ — the product and the tool itself.
- https://upscalify.theatom.lk/about — what Upscalify is, who built it, and its
  relationship to The Atom.

- Maker: Zaki
- Studio: The Atom (https://theatom.lk)
